body: For reference, `@typescript-eslint/no-non-null-assertion` is almost always because of mistyped DB collections. I know how to fix the typing but agreed with Omar to defer until we finish UM.

body:
QueryBuilder is always very clunky and should be last resort imo, suggestion:
```ts
const entries = await Db.collections.SharedWorkflow!.find({ user: deletedUser })`
const sharedWorkflowIds = entries.map(entry => entry.workflowId)
await Db.collections.Workflow!.delete(sharedWorkflowIds)
await Db.collections.SharedWorkflow!.delete({ user: deletedUser })
```
(naming just mocked)

doing the same for the credentials and we have it done in 8 lines of code 👍🏼

body: Is this storing half of the user's password in clear text in the JWT payload? 🤔 If so, I'd advise to store a salted hash instead.
author: csuermann

body: Added clarification comment
author: krynble

body: We are now adding md5 to the user passwords's hash half so it's not plain text.
author: krynble

body: @krynble NIST has [deprecated](https://tools.ietf.org/id/draft-lvelvindron-tls-md5-sha1-deprecate-01.html) MD5 and SHA1. As this is dealing with password data, can I suggest using at least SHA-256? Ideally a modern key derivation function (KDF) like [argon2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argon2) or [scrypt](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scrypt).
author: pemontto

body: amazing job 👍🏼
author: BHesseldieck

body:
I think the `Math.round` is rounding always up for it, maybe we should then use `Math.ceil`?

Why don't we omit it at all? Slice would use the Integer (floor) number of it, wouldn't this be fine too?
author: BHesseldieck

body: ref previous comment about `Math.round`
author: BHesseldieck

body:
Thanks for bringing this up - in fact, it does get the integer part only. I changed to `Math.ceil` to make it clear that we're always rounding up not to generate confusion.

The reason not to omit is that more characters is always better than less. Esp for shorter strings, it makes a lot of difference, so I think using ceil works best.
author: krynble

body:
If you want to have more than you should use `Math.floor` or even omit the rounding, since slice will round down and take the integer value of it example:

```js
var password = '1234567891011'
password.slice(Math.ceil(password.length / 2)) --> '891011'
password.slice(Math.floor(password.length / 2)) --> '7891011'
password.slice(password.length / 2) --> '7891011'
```
author: BHesseldieck

body: Maybe we should use `await this.helpers.prepareBinaryData(message.value as Buffer);` instead ?
author: d3rwan

body: How do you want to use the data? what type of data are you expecting to get when you use useRawMessage?
author: RicardoE105

body:
In my use-case, data in the kafka topic is protobuf and with the nominal behaviour (original data is stringified), when I try to deserialize the protobuf, it doesn't work. 
That's why I would like keep the raw data to use it in another node which deserialize my protobuf data.
author: d3rwan

body:
Hey @d3rwan,

Do you happen to have an example workflow that shows how you would use this?
author: Joffcom

body: @d3rwan any updates on this one? Just trying to get my head around it :) It seems like a good idea to have but I am wondering if it is generic enough would there ever be a case to have a raw message that isn't a buffer or a prefered binary data object?
author: Joffcom

body:
@Joffcom sorry for the delay ! 

It's not easy to share an example workflow because I obviously have specific node which read encoded protobuf and parse it it JSON, and I can't share it here. 

But my workflow is like that : 
```
{ "nodes": [ { "parameters": { "topic": "identity-events", "groupId": "n8n-client-id", "options": {} }, "name": "Kafka Trigger", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.kafkaTrigger", "typeVersion": 1, "position": [ 460, 300 ], "credentials": { "kafka": { "id": "1", "name": "Kafka account" } } }, { "parameters": { "message": "message", "targetField": "identityEvent" }, "name": "Identity Event Decoder", "type": "@swan-io/n8n-nodes-swan.identityEventDecoder", "typeVersion": 1, "position": [ 680, 300 ] }, { "parameters": {}, "name": "NoOp", "type": "n8n-nodes-base.noOp", "typeVersion": 1, "position": [ 900, 300 ] } ], "connections": { "Kafka Trigger": { "main": [ [ { "node": "Identity Event Decoder", "type": "main", "index": 0 } ] ] }, "Identity Event Decoder": { "main": [ [ { "node": "NoOp", "type": "main", "index": 0 } ] ] } } }

```

The node `Identity Event Decoder` will transform a protobuf buffer (I use https://github.com/stephenh/ts-proto for encoding/decoding) in a JS object. 

And with the default behaviour, I think the toString method can alter buffer sometimes, and then the decoding no longer works
author: d3rwan

body:
Hey @d3rwan,

So at the moment this change would potentially only be useful for someone who has the extra node you have? It seems like a good feature to add but testing is possibly tricky :)

I will have a chat with the team internally to see what they want to do with this one.
author: Joffcom
